rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe copy at line 11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that argv data can exceed the 256-byte destination, causing a global-buffer-overflow. They explicitly named it as a global-buffer-overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with explicit null-termination but did not include rejecting overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input, which was not provided.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended using bounded string APIs (strlcpy/snprintf) and implementing centralized input validation before copying into fixed storage, which removes raw unbounded string handling from the data path. This satisfies the root-cause/architectural fix requirement.
